name: Rosalind Russell
abstract: Catherine Rosalind Russell (June 4, 1907 – November 28, 1976) was an American actress, model, comedian, screenwriter, and singer, known for her role as fast-talking newspaper reporter Hildy Johnson in the Howard Hawks screwball comedy His Girl Friday (1940), opposite Cary Grant, as well as for her role of catty Sylvia Fowler in George Cu…

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Her name was Rosalind Russell. She left when she graduated from high school. She went to Washington and married some politician or someone. And she had told Byas about me.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: So we practically knew all of the motion picture stars of that day... Joan Crawford used to be out, Rosalind Russell.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: I played in the background music in that picture, with Rosalind Russell.
